verses.text: Ishmael, of royal blood, comes to Gedaliah, eats his bread, then rises and murders him with his men. Treachery wears a friendly face. The basest betrayals often come from those we have welcomed to our table.

Ishmael, of royal blood, comes to Gedaliah, eats his bread, then rises and murders him with his men. Treachery wears a friendly face. The basest betrayals often come from those we have welcomed to our table.

Ishmael, of royal blood, comes to Gedaliah, eats his bread, then rises and murders him with his men. Treachery wears a friendly face. The basest betrayals often come from those we have welcomed to our table.
The next day he slaughters fourscore mourners coming with offerings, casting them into a pit. Wanton cruelty knows no bounds. A heart set on evil spares neither the innocent nor the worshipper.
The next day he slaughters fourscore mourners coming with offerings, casting them into a pit. Wanton cruelty knows no bounds. A heart set on evil spares neither the innocent nor the worshipper.
The next day he slaughters fourscore mourners coming with offerings, casting them into a pit. Wanton cruelty knows no bounds. A heart set on evil spares neither the innocent nor the worshipper.
The next day he slaughters fourscore mourners coming with offerings, casting them into a pit. Wanton cruelty knows no bounds. A heart set on evil spares neither the innocent nor the worshipper.
Ten buy their lives with hidden stores; the rest he carries off captive. Greed stays his hand where pity could not. Even among murderers, God can use a base motive to spare a remnant.
Ten buy their lives with hidden stores; the rest he carries off captive. Greed stays his hand where pity could not. Even among murderers, God can use a base motive to spare a remnant.
Ten buy their lives with hidden stores; the rest he carries off captive. Greed stays his hand where pity could not. Even among murderers, God can use a base motive to spare a remnant.
Johanan pursues, and the captives are glad to see him. Rescue appears. God does not leave the oppressed without hope of deliverance.
Johanan pursues, and the captives are glad to see him. Rescue appears. God does not leave the oppressed without hope of deliverance.
Johanan pursues, and the captives are glad to see him. Rescue appears. God does not leave the oppressed without hope of deliverance.
The people turn to Johanan, but Ishmael escapes to the Ammonites. The wicked slips away for now. Justice may seem to lag, but God's reckoning is sure.
The people turn to Johanan, but Ishmael escapes to the Ammonites. The wicked slips away for now. Justice may seem to lag, but God's reckoning is sure.
Johanan leads the remnant toward Egypt, afraid of the Chaldeans after Gedaliah's death. Fear drives them the wrong way. Panic, not faith, now shapes their steps, and it leads them from God's land.
